title: SystemVerilog support enhancement request (structure literals)
body:
---


Author Name: **Ed Lander**
Original Redmine Issue: 618 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2013-02-12
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

Hi,


We've noticed that Verilator is unhappy with the following syntax:

const struct_type constant_name = struct_type'{ enable : 1'h0, address : 32'h00000000 };

-not expecting the structure literal in front of the '

This also applies to the following function call (where the two parameters are over-riding the feature field of the structure):

assign wire_name = function_name(struct_type'{ feature : { 6'd0, parameter_1, parameter_2 }, version : constant_name.version }),

The literal is optional, but improves readability (particularly for the latter example); if it made it easier for Verilator we'd be happy with a restriction where they must all be declared explicitly.


Cheers,
Ed
